# UC-21.7.1 · Baggage Handling System Throughput and Misroute Detection

## Description

Low BHS throughput and rising misroutes delay connections and drive mishandled-bag metrics; correlating belt rates with sort errors focuses maintenance on diverters and scanners.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from Baggage Handling System Throughput and Misroute Detection to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Parse BSM/BUM messages or vendor XML into normalized fields. Validate `sort_destination` against flight plan lookups when available. Alert BHS control for sustained misroute rates above SLA. **Domain context:** IATA RP 1745 baggage messaging; mishandled-bag KPIs drive airline contracts—misroutes also stress rescreening/recirc paths. **Splunk:** `bags < 50` filters low-volume belts—tune for small terminals.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Airport Ground Operations App, BHS message feed via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=airport` `sourcetype="airport:baggage"` (flight_id, bag_tag, sort_destination, actual_destination, belt_id, scan_time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Parse BSM/BUM messages or vendor XML into normalized fields. Validate `sort_destination` against flight plan lookups when available. Alert BHS control for sustained misroute rates above SLA. **Domain context:** IATA RP 1745 baggage messaging; mishandled-bag KPIs drive airline contracts—misroutes also stress rescreening/recirc paths. **Splunk:** `bags < 50` filters low-volume belts—tune for small terminals.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=airport sourcetype="airport:baggage"
| eval misroute=if(sort_destination!=actual_destination,1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as bags, sum(misroute) as misroutes, dc(bag_tag) as unique_bags by belt_id, _time
| eval misroute_pct=round(100*misroutes/nullif(bags,0),2)
| where misroute_pct > 2 OR bags < 50
| table _time, belt_id, bags, misroutes, misroute_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Baggage Handling System Throughput and Misroute Detection** — Low BHS throughput and rising misroutes delay connections and drive mishandled-bag metrics; correlating belt rates with sort errors focuses maintenance on diverters and scanners.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=airport` `sourcetype="airport:baggage"` (flight_id, bag_tag, sort_destination, actual_destination, belt_id, scan_time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Airport Ground Operations App, BHS message feed via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: airport; **sourcetype**: airport:baggage. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=airport, sourcetype="airport:baggage". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **misroute** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by belt_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **misroute_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where misroute_pct > 2 OR bags < 50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Baggage Handling System Throughput and Misroute Detection**): table _time, belt_id, bags, misroutes, misroute_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Compare alert rows with the airport operations or A-CDM tool and, where relevant, airline systems for the same flight or stand. Validate identifiers (flight, stand, belt) match your lookup tables.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (bags per belt), Bar chart (misroute %), Sankey (planned vs actual sort, if supported).

## SPL

```spl
index=airport sourcetype="airport:baggage"
| eval misroute=if(sort_destination!=actual_destination,1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as bags, sum(misroute) as misroutes, dc(bag_tag) as unique_bags by belt_id, _time
| eval misroute_pct=round(100*misroutes/nullif(bags,0),2)
| where misroute_pct > 2 OR bags < 50
| table _time, belt_id, bags, misroutes, misroute_pct
```

## Visualization

Time chart (bags per belt), Bar chart (misroute %), Sankey (planned vs actual sort, if supported).
